evidence: "The top features were defined as the highest average normalized hits across 100 iterations" and the random forest importance (Z-scores) are evaluated and compared with shuffled replicas to determine importance. The paper makes biomarker selections based on importance ranking: "these consistently high-importance taxa are good candidates for disease-related microbial biomarkers." While ANCOM-BC differential abundance provides some validation for CRC and CD, this corroboration is incomplete and fails entirely for immunotherapy ("ANCOM-BC did not identify any genus and species-level taxa as being differentially abundant"), yet the importance-based biomarker claims remain central to the conclusions without reliance on permutation, SHAP, PDP/ALE, conditional importance, or held-out validation.
